criteria.label: State H₀ and H₁ for a given scenario and identify one- vs two-sided alternatives.
criteria.example: Latency test: H₀: μ = 200 ms vs H₁: μ < 200 ms (one-sided) because only decreases matter operationally.

criteria.label: Compute a one-sample t statistic by hand and interpret its sign and magnitude.
criteria.example: x̄=94, μ₀=100, s=10, n=25 → t=(94−100)/(10/5)=−3.0: sample mean is 3 standard errors below μ₀.

criteria.label: Explain p-value, Type I error, and Type II error without conflating them.
criteria.example: p=0.04: rare data if H₀ true. Type I: reject true H₀ (α). Type II: miss false H₀ (β). p is not P(H₀|data).

criteria.label: Run and interpret a t-test in Python, pairing results with a confidence interval.
criteria.example: Use `stats.ttest_1samp` and `stats.t.interval`; if 95% CI excludes μ₀, two-sided α=0.05 test rejects H₀.

criteria.label: Choose an appropriate test (one-sample, paired, two-sample Welch) for an ML experiment design.
criteria.example: Same users rate two models → paired t on differences; independent cohorts with unequal variance → Welch `ttest_ind(..., equal_var=False)`.
